channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Vote expected on Iowa bill that would remove gender identity protections from civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Amid a heavy police presence and dozens of vocal protesters, Iowa lawmakers were expected to vote Thursday on an unprecedented bill stripping the state civil rights code of protections based on gender identity, a move opponents say could expose transgender people to discrimin
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board 'historic' Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Neighbor shoots man accused of stabbing woman in Brentwood: Authorities
item.description: Authorities are investigating a stabbing that led to a shooting Wednesday afternoon in a Brentwood neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: GOP Sen. Mike Lee suggests letting ‘pirates’ take on cartels on the border
item.description: He floated the idea that Congress and President Trump should allow private citizens, or "pirates," to take on international cartels to protect national security.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Wife of murdered Minnesota pastor charged in alleged plot to kill husband on mission trip: church
item.description: Church officials say the wife of a Minnesota missionary is officially facing charges in his killing of her husband in a murder-to-hire plot in Angola.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Trump's deadline to eliminate DEI nears, few schools openly rush to make changes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools and colleges across the U.S. face a Friday deadline to end diversity programs or risk having their federal money pulled by the Trump administration, yet few are openly rushing to make changes.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, JOCELYN GECKER and CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican lawmakers look for ways to weaken state judges
item.description: TOPEKA, Kansas (AP) — Measures in several state legislatures this year have called for new approaches to weaken the power of judges. One would abandon a centuries-old precedent that courts can decide whether laws are constitutional. Another would change how judges are selected.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote in an op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Navy widow's long wait for burial at sea about to end
item.description: Wanda Davis never dreamed it would take her 14 months to say the words "now we can have some closure and move on.
item.dc:creator: Chris Horne, NewsNation

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, KRON4

item.title: USAID employees given 15 minutes to clear out offices
item.description: U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees were notified late Tuesday they would be given 15 minutes to collect their belongings from their offices as the Trump administration forges ahead with gutting the agency.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Utah is one step away from becoming 1st state to ban fluoride in water
item.description: The Utah governor's signature is all that stands between a state ban on water fluoridation after House Bill 81 cleared its final hurdle in the legislature on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, KRON4

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: The Trump administration will be revamping the U.S. investor visa, increasing the funds required to secure the status while dubbing the new option a "gold card.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: With a push from President Donald Trump, House Republicans sent a GOP budget blueprint to passage Tuesday, a step toward delivering his “big, beautiful bill” with $4.5 trillion in tax breaks and $2 trillion in spending cuts despite a wall of opposition from Democrats and discomfort among Republicans
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: A federal judge in Seattle blocks Trump’s effort to halt the refugee admissions system
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge in Seattle on Tuesday blocked President Donald Trump’s suspension of the nation’s refugee admissions system, saying that while the president has broad authority over who comes into the country, he cannot nullify the law passed by Congress establishing the program.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign over Musk's demands
item.description: In a resignation letter, the tech workers said they wouldn't help "dismantle critical public services," as Elon Musk and DOGE try to cut the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the Feb. 28 Economic Blackout
item.description: Customers across the United States are looking to send a message Friday to companies that have rolled back their DEI initiatives in the form of a 24-hour consumer boycott, also known as the “Feb. 28 Economic Blackout.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, ABC 4

item.title: As DeSantis talks up his wife as next Florida governor, here's what to know about Casey
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The aspirations of Florida’s first lady Casey DeSantis have long been a topic of discussion in the state capital of Tallahassee. This week, her husband Gov. Ron DeSantis added to the speculation by talking her up as his potential successor.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Funeral set for an officer killed while responding to a gunman in a Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A funeral service will be held later this week for a Pennsylvania police officer shot and killed while responding to a hospital intensive care unit where authorities said a gunman had shot three hospital employees and taken staff members hostage last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: 11 dead after supplemental shakes linked to listeria cases in 21 states
item.description: Frozen shakes sold to nursing homes and other institutions are being recalled after a listeria outbreak in 21 states that has caused 11 deaths, according to the Federal Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Johnston, WGN 9
